column1: # Schema — vocabulary.csv
# Structured glossary for an atom. Every row = one term.
# First non-comment line is the header row. Comments start with '#' and are
# preserved when editing by hand (tools should round-trip them).
# Fields:
#   term           Canonical term as students will see it in book.md.
#                  Lowercase unless it's a proper noun or always-capitalized
#                  abbreviation (e.g.
#   definition     1-2 sentence precise definition. Avoid circular definitions.
#   part_of_speech noun | verb | adjective | adverb | abbreviation | compound
#   example        One short sentence using the term in context.
#                  Should sound like it could appear in book.md.
#   source_key     Matches a key in sources.md. Blank if common knowledge.
#   related_atoms  Semicolon-separated atom_ids whose sidecars also cover
#                  this term. Blank if term is atom-local.
#   difficulty     1 | 2 | 3 — roughly the atom's difficulty when the term
#                  is first introduced. Drives flashcard order.
# Sizing guidance: 20-60 terms for most atoms; up to 80 for survey atoms.
term
<replace-this-row>
